title: The Ghosts Betwixt - A Prototype Preview
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MxDzq8aLzTo

Daniel (@dungeondive) previews a prototype of The Ghosts Betwixt, a dungeon crawl from designer Dustin Frond, made as a favour to an online adventure-gaming friend. The hook is the setting: a modern-day haunted-house crawl where you don't play heroes but a family — mother Joan, father Bill, and kids Avalyn and Maddox — breaking into the Bennert family's rural farmhouse to rescue kidnapped son Richie. Daniel pitches the tone as Maniac Mansion meets the Hardy Boys meets Scooby-Doo, with a dash of Texas Chainsaw Massacre 2, wrapped in bright, cartoony Saturday-morning art unlike anything else in the genre. Exploration runs on stacks of map tokens and fate tokens: reveal a tile, draw three fates to seed treasure, monsters, ambushes and story events, then split the stacks at branching doors — Warhammer Quest-style, where the path you choose matters. He's most taken with the monster targeting system, where drawn tokens fix which family member each enemy group hunts, and with the household-junk arsenal (slingshots, a cracked mop, a TV-dinner tray) equipped against a time budget. His verdict: a theme-first design where mechanisms and fiction reinforce each other, not a reskin.

----

Playing a family instead of a party of heroes is a rare framing — does casting the crawl as parents rescuing their kid change how you'd feel about the danger, or is a dungeon still a dungeon?
